D3DPERF_DumpPerfEvents

Displays a subset of the Direct3D performance events currently stored in the perf-event buffer.

void WINAPI D3DPERF_DumpPerfEvents();

Parameters

None.

Return Values

None.

Remarks

The set of data displayed can be controlled using the D3DPerfEventSpewFrameStart and D3DPerfEventSpewFrameCount members of the D3DPERF structure

The output might look like the following:

  Event                  CpuStart   CpuTime  GpuStart  GpuTime  CpuPut   Data Gpu%
  Kickoff                       0      1683         0        0  113320      0
     *** FrameMove ***
  User0                     12705    106084   1900673   109887  113376   6176
  DrawIndexedVertices      108691      6869   1947551    54773  115132   3924   54/86
  DrawIndexedVertices      123267    278445   2216913  1471443  120420  86052

In the output above, FrameMove is a user-defined performance event that was inserted by the title using D3DPERF_InsertPerfEventHeader. The User0 event was inserted using code such as:

D3DPERFEvent *pD3DPerfEvent = D3DPERF_PerfEventStart( D3DPERFEvent_User0, TRUE );
...
D3DPERF_PerfEventEnd( pD3DPerfEvent, TRUE );

Note    The header and user event above could have been inserted using a single call to D3DPERF_BlockTimer.

The following table describes the meaning of each of the column headers in the D3DPERF_DumpPerfEvents output:

Column Description
CpuStart Time at which the CPU started processing the event, measured in CPU cycles since the start of the D3DPERF_DumpPerfEvents spew.
CpuTime Amount of time the CPU spent processing the event, measured in CPU cycles.
GpuStart Time at which the GPU started processing the event, measured in CPU cycles since the start of the D3DPERF_DumpPerfEvents spew. Note that 'CpuStart' and 'GpuStart' are on the same time scale, so a given time T represents the same wall-clock time in both columns.
GpuTime Amount of time the GPU spent processing the event, measured in CPU cycles.
CpuPut Offset at which CPU was writing into push buffer at start of event, measured relative to start of push buffer.
Gpu% Percentage of time the GPU front-end and back-end was busy between the start of the last event and the start of this event. If the GPU has not been sampled enough times since the last event, no value will be output, and those samples will be considered along with the next event.

Instead of manually calling D3DPERF_DumpPerfEvents, a title can set the D3DPERF_DUMP_FPS_PERFEVENTS flag in the m_dwDumpFPSInfoMask field of the D3DPERF struct. That will cause D3DPERF_DumpFrameRateInfo to also call D3DPERF_DumpPerfEvents after it displays its other performance information.

D3DPERF_DumpPerfEvents uses the following members in the D3DPERF structure when determining what information to output:

  • D3DPerfEventSpewFrameStart
  • D3DPerfEventSpewFrameCount
  • m_DumpPerfCycleTimesThresholdCpu
  • m_DumpPerfCycleTimesThresholdGpu

Note    Calling D3DPERF_StartCountingPerfEvent will initialize D3DPerfEventSpewFrameStart and D3DPerfEventSpewFrameCount but does not modify the other two members. m_DumpPerfCycleTimesThresholdCpu and m_DumpPerfCycleTimesThresholdGpu will be zero unless the title sets to something else.

Requirements

Header: Declared in D3d8perf.h.

Import Library: Use D3d8i.lib or d3d8d.lib.

See Also

D3DPERF_StartCountingPerfEvent, D3DPERF_PerfEventStart
